mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits combined with expanding retail sales indicate synchronized housing and consumer demand shocks that boost corporate revenue forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample windows spanning 2000-2020.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, signaling supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and trigger equity sell-offs over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where INDPRO growth turns negative while CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative increase in the CPI index exceeding 2.5 percent signals accelerating inflation expectations that force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return distribution shows no statistically significant negative skew when CPIAUCSL three-month changes exceed 2.5 percent (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, which systematically lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all available post-1980 data windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable federal funds rate indicate a healthy labor market operating without monetary restraint, which sustains earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and flat FEDFUNDS fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months triggers recession fears that force multiple compression and earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window.
